message:
refactor(mvcc): replace registry-wide Mutex with per-handle Arc<Mutex<ConcurrentHandle>>

The ConcurrentRegistry previously stored ConcurrentHandle values
directly, requiring callers to hold the entire registry mutex while
reading or mutating any single handle.  This created unnecessary
contention: every VDBE read_page, write_page, free_page, and witness
recording call had to lock the full registry, look up the session, and
hold the guard for the duration of the operation.

This change wraps each handle in Arc<Mutex<ConcurrentHandle>>
(SharedConcurrentHandle) so callers can clone the Arc once at VDBE
setup time and lock only their own handle for each operation.

Key changes across crates:

fsqlite-mvcc/begin_concurrent — Registry stores
HashMap<u64, SharedConcurrentHandle>.  `iter_active()` now yields
cloned Arcs. `handle(session_id)` returns an Option<SharedConcurrentHandle>
for long-lived references.  `get()`/`get_mut()` return locked guards for
short-lived access.  `HandleView` is now fully owned (snapshot of token,
begin_seq, read_pages, tracked_write_pages, witness keys, and SSI edge
flags) so SSI validation never holds a handle lock.  Free-function
signatures (`concurrent_write_page`, `concurrent_free_page`,
`concurrent_abort`, `concurrent_rollback_to_savepoint`, etc.) accept
`impl DerefMut<Target = ConcurrentHandle>` instead of `&mut ConcurrentHandle`,
keeping them generic over both owned guards and direct references.
Added fast-path in `concurrent_write_page` that skips lock acquisition
and duplicate witness recording when a page is already owned.
Two new tests: write-page fast-path deduplication and
registry-remove-keeps-handle-alive.

fsqlite-mvcc/lib — Re-export `SharedConcurrentHandle`.

fsqlite-btree/cursor — Add `write_page_data` to the `PageWriter` trait
with a default delegation to `write_page` plus an optimized override in
`TransactionPageIo` and `MemPageStore` that avoids copying owned
PageData back through a borrowed slice.  All balance call sites
(`balance_deeper`, `balance_quick`, `balance_nonroot`,
`balance_shallower`, `split_overflowing_root`) and cursor insert/delete
paths now call `write_page_data` with `PageData::from_vec`.
New test covers the owned-data write path.

fsqlite-btree/balance — Switched all `write_page` calls to
`write_page_data` with `PageData::from_vec`.

fsqlite-pager/traits — Add `write_page_data` to `TransactionHandle`
with default delegation, plus `MemoryMockTransaction` override that
inserts PageData directly.

fsqlite-pager/pager — Introduce `StagedPage::from_page_data` backed
by a new `StagedPageBacking::Owned` variant so that btree-produced
page buffers skip the PageBuf round-trip entirely.  `published_page()`
uses `OnceLock` for lazy one-shot materialization.  `into_buf()` now
takes a pool reference to convert owned data back when needed.
Extracted `durable_freelist_pages_with_inner`,
`committed_durable_freelist_pages_with_inner`, and
`predicted_durable_freelist_pages_with_inner` helpers so
`freelist_metadata_dirty` can compare actual durable freelist state
rather than using the coarse "any freed or allocated" heuristic, which
previously caused spurious page-1 rewrites on net-zero
allocate-then-free cycles.  Three new tests verify net-zero freelist
reuse, net-zero EOF allocate/free, and concurrent reuse-then-free
beyond-db-size pages produce no WAL frames.

fsqlite-core/connection — VDBE setup now clones a
SharedConcurrentHandle once and passes it through
ConcurrentExecContext instead of cloning the full registry Arc.
SSI snapshot collection and conflict evidence gathering lock individual
handles.

fsqlite-vdbe/engine — ConcurrentContext stores a
SharedConcurrentHandle instead of Arc<Mutex<ConcurrentRegistry>>.
All registry lock/get/get_mut sequences replaced with direct
`ctx.handle.lock()`.  Write-page and free-page busy-retry loops now
perform the MVCC mutation inside the same lock scope as the
conflict-seq check, eliminating the previous two-phase lock pattern
that could miss concurrent state changes between unlock and re-lock.
Updated test setup to use registered sessions with proper handles.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -55,11 +55,12 @@ mod ssi_anomaly_tests;
 
 pub use begin_concurrent::{
     ConcurrentHandle, ConcurrentPageState, ConcurrentRegistry, ConcurrentSavepoint, FcwResult,
-    MAX_CONCURRENT_WRITERS, PreparedConcurrentCommit, SsiResult, concurrent_abort,
-    concurrent_commit, concurrent_commit_with_ssi, concurrent_free_page, concurrent_page_is_freed,
-    concurrent_page_state, concurrent_read_page, concurrent_restore_page_state,
-    concurrent_rollback_to_savepoint, concurrent_savepoint, concurrent_track_write_conflict_page,
-    concurrent_write_page, finalize_prepared_concurrent_commit_with_ssi, is_concurrent_mode,
+    MAX_CONCURRENT_WRITERS, PreparedConcurrentCommit, SharedConcurrentHandle, SsiResult,
+    concurrent_abort, concurrent_commit, concurrent_commit_with_ssi, concurrent_free_page,
+    concurrent_page_is_freed, concurrent_page_state, concurrent_read_page,
+    concurrent_restore_page_state, concurrent_rollback_to_savepoint, concurrent_savepoint,
+    concurrent_track_write_conflict_page, concurrent_write_page,
+    finalize_prepared_concurrent_commit_with_ssi, is_concurrent_mode,
     prepare_concurrent_commit_with_ssi, validate_first_committer_wins,
 };
 pub use bocpd::{BocpdConfig, BocpdMonitor, ConjugateModel, HazardFunction, RegimeStats};
